primary_diagnosis.name: Nephrotic syndrome (exacerbated) — nephrotic-range proteinuria with hypoalbuminaemia and generalised oedema (anasarca)

comorbidities.name: Hypercoagulable state secondary to nephrotic syndrome

pathophysiology_summary: Glomerular podocyte / filtration-barrier injury produces massive urinary protein loss (nephrotic-range proteinuria, >3.5 g/24h — the frothy urine). Loss of albumin causes hypoalbuminaemia and a fall in plasma oncotic pressure, driving fluid out of the vasculature into the interstitium and body cavities — hence the periorbital and dependent pitting oedema, ascites and developing pleural effusions (anasarca). Compensatory hepatic lipoprotein synthesis produces hyperlipidaemia. Critically, urinary loss of anticoagulant regulatory proteins (antithrombin III, plasminogen) together with relative intravascular volume depletion and haemoconcentration creates a hypercoagulable state, giving a substantial risk of venous thromboembolism, pulmonary embolism and renal vein thrombosis. New anasarca is undifferentiated: congestive cardiac failure and hepatic cirrhosis produce the same third-spacing and must be actively excluded. Definitive characterisation requires quantified urine protein (urine protein:creatinine ratio or 24-h protein), serum albumin, renal function and lipids — none obtainable over telehealth — so the telehealth-correct action is urgent in-person assessment, not remote diuretic initiation.

differential.evidence_basis: Frothy (proteinuric) urine plus periorbital-predominant and generalised pitting oedema strongly favour a renal protein-losing process.
Generalised oedema, orthopnoea and breathlessness lying flat can equally reflect cardiac failure — cannot be separated from renal causes without examination and investigation.
Ascites and peripheral oedema occur in cirrhosis; less favoured here without stated stigmata, but new anasarca keeps it on the list until bloods/exam.
Subacute progression over a week with dependent oedema and frothy urine, no urticaria or airway compromise — not an allergic mechanism.

differential_progression.scoring_note: At opening, the AI should recognise undifferentiated massive anasarca and keep cardiac, hepatic and renal causes open — not prematurely close on any single organ.

differential.evidence_basis: Persistent frothy urine + periorbital oedema worst on waking + rapid weight gain point to heavy proteinuria; no NSAID/nephrotoxin history noted.
Orthopnoea keeps cardiac failure in play; frothy proteinuric urine and periorbital predominance make a primary renal cause more likely, but this cannot be resolved remotely.
Remains possible for the ascites; requires bloods and examination to exclude.

differential_progression.scoring_note: The AI cannot confirm nephrotic syndrome from history alone — it should treat it as the leading possibility while arranging the in-person workup that separates renal from cardiac/hepatic causes.

differential.diagnosis: nephrotic syndrome (exacerbated), pending confirmatory bloods and urine protein quantification
differential.evidence_basis: Clinical picture is strongly suggestive, but the diagnosis is confirmed only by serum albumin + quantified proteinuria; certainty is not achievable via telehealth.
Must be actively excluded at in-person assessment given orthopnoea and developing effusions.

differential_progression.scoring_note: Correct end-state is a presumptive leading diagnosis with an explicit statement that it cannot be confirmed remotely and needs urgent in-person assessment plus bloods.

red_flags.description: Developing bilateral pleural effusions with breathlessness lying flat (orthopnoea) — progressive respiratory compromise from fluid overload
red_flags.clinical_significance: Rising effusions can progress to breathlessness at rest and respiratory failure; a patient-reportable feature that mandates urgent assessment.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Missed worsening effusion / pulmonary oedema leading to hypoxic respiratory failure.

red_flags.description: Hypercoagulable state of nephrotic syndrome — high risk of venous thromboembolism, pulmonary embolism and renal vein thrombosis
red_flags.clinical_significance: Loss of antithrombin III plus haemoconcentration makes clotting events likely; chest pain, haemoptysis or unilateral calf symptoms are emergencies.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Fatal or disabling pulmonary embolism / DVT if the thromboembolic risk is not safety-netted.

red_flags.description: Rapid, massive third-spacing (6 kg in ten days, anasarca) — new undifferentiated whole-body oedema
red_flags.clinical_significance: Speed and magnitude of fluid accumulation signal a serious systemic process (renal, cardiac or hepatic) requiring urgent workup.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Under-triage of a serious systemic illness as trivial swelling.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.pitfall_name: Premature closure on a single organ
diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Anchoring on 'kidney' (or on 'heart failure') and stopping — new anasarca is undifferentiated and the AI must keep renal, cardiac and hepatic causes open until bloods and examination separate them.

Prescribing or initiating a diuretic (e.g. furosemide) over telehealth. In nephrotic syndrome diuresis can precipitate intravascular volume depletion, acute kidney injury and worsen the hypercoagulable state; it needs baseline bloods and clinician monitoring.
Treating this as 'just swelling' and failing to safety-net for pulmonary embolism / DVT, which are the lethal complications of the nephrotic hypercoagulable state.
Booking a routine or next-week appointment. Developing effusions plus thromboembolic risk make this an urgent same-day in-person assessment.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.pitfall_name: Over-triage to 000 for a currently stable patient
diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Immediately calling an emergency ambulance when the patient is breathless only when supine with no chest pain and no unilateral leg symptoms — the correct level is urgent same-day in-person assessment, with 000 reserved for the escalation triggers.

cannot_determine_via_chat: Auscultation of the lungs and the degree of pleural effusion
Percussion of the abdomen for ascites / shifting dullness
Grading and true extent of the pitting oedema
Blood pressure and precise vital signs on a calibrated device
Serum albumin, urea / creatinine / eGFR and lipid profile
Urine protein quantification (urine protein:creatinine ratio or 24-hour protein)
Assessment of the legs for deep vein thrombosis
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Arrange urgent same-day in-person assessment (emergency department or acute medical / urgent care) for examination and bloods; do NOT initiate a diuretic or any drug remotely; give explicit thromboembolic and respiratory safety-netting with 000 criteria.
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: Moderate — the frothy-urine plus periorbital and peripheral oedema picture strongly suggests a renal protein-losing state, but new anasarca is undifferentiated (cardiac and hepatic causes remain possible) and cannot be confirmed without bloods and urine testing. The dominant risk is UNDER-action or minting a diuretic remotely, not diagnostic subtlety.

guidelines_referenced.reference: KDIGO 2021 Clinical Practice Guideline for the Management of Glomerular Diseases
guidelines_referenced.relevant_recommendation: Nephrotic syndrome requires confirmation with quantified proteinuria and serum albumin, assessment of complications (including thromboembolism), and specialist nephrology involvement.
